# Transcrição: Conversion Tracking

- **Fonte:** Conversion Tracking
- **Duração:** 08:53
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

## [00:00 - 03:00]

G'day, welcome back. It's Mike from agenciesheavy.com and in this module of AdWords Fundamentals, we're going to cover conversion tracking. Super, super important. Please don't skip this. Yes, there's a little bit of code involved here, but I'm going to show you how to cope with that. But very, very important. Let me tell you a quick story. I did an audit earlier this week with a man who shall remain nameless in South Africa running a very, very successful business. He's running the AdWords account in his own business now because he's had a couple of agencies have a crack at it. I think he mentioned, well, I'd better not mention names, but big, big agencies, famous advertising agencies that you would have heard of. And they did a worse job than he did. So we took it back over and he was running it better than they were. And when I had a look inside the account a few days ago, I saw that nothing was being tracked. No conversions were being tracked. Now a conversion is just the measure of success. How do we know when this thing's working? How do we know when people are doing what we want them to do? And I'll give you a couple of examples of that in a second, but he had no conversion tracking set up. So he had no way of knowing and he's spending maybe, I don't know, 15 grand a month or so and has been doing that for quite a while. No way of knowing which keywords are good or bad, which adds a good or bad, which campaigns are good or bad. No way of knowing which clicks were leading to people doing what he wants on the website versus those that weren't. And therefore, no way of optimizing. Now the reason I tell you that whole story is not to pick on him, but because he said something to me on that phone call that I couldn't believe. He said, and all this time I've been doing this with all these other agencies that have had a crack at this, you're the first person to ever talk to me about conversion tracking, which utterly blew me away. That these big agencies that have been charging him money to run this AdWords account didn't even have the conversation with him about conversion tracking. So by the end of this module, I want you to be in the top probably 20% of advertisers because you're going to have these conversations with your clients and make sure that stuff is being tracked. You cannot improve an AdWords account where you don't have some kind of conversion tracking. And again, a conversion is just getting people to do what we want them to do. There's four main types of conversion. It's getting people to do stuff on our website. Apps, phone calls and importing conversions from another system are way beyond the scope of this particular course. So I'm just going to focus on the website stuff. And generally, when we're talking about getting people to do stuff on our website, if you're a retailer, it's getting people to buy. Well, thank you, Mike. Well done. Thanks for that. So tracking how many people made it to

## [03:00 - 06:01]

the receipt page at the end of your shopping cart, that's going to be the thing that we track as a conversion. If you're a lead gen site, then it's how many people filled out that form for the free consultation or whatever it is that you offer. Now, there are other things you could track on there like phone calls, how many people called us, how many people clicked on the link to the PDF spec sheet that we've got all over our site. How many people clicked the button to disappear off to our Facebook page or how many people engaged with us in the live chat or send us a Facebook message with our brand new shiny chat bot. There's all sorts of things that you could track. I'm going to assume for now that the main thing that you're going to track is people reaching a particular page on your website. So again, for lead gen, that's typically going to be the thank you page that people see once they've filled out that form. And for e-com sites, that's generally going to be the receipt page that somebody sees after they've paid you money and gone successfully through your shopping cart. There are a couple of things here. So once you go in, you'll find the conversions in your upwards account and you click on the website option. These are the options that you have to fill out. So let's quickly walk through those. You need to give your conversion a name. Again, something relatively short, you don't need to use all 100 characters here, but something that's going to make sense to you and anybody else that might be looking inside the account. You get to give it a category that's not super important. You're probably going to choose lead or sale. There's a list of about five in there. It doesn't really matter. It doesn't affect anything. It's just for your reference. Value, I think is super important. But for now, if you're a lead gen site, use the first option, use the same value for each conversion and just put a number in there. It doesn't really matter what the number is as long as you don't use zero. So you can put one, you can say each lead is worth one point to me or each lead is worth 100 points to me. It really doesn't matter where this comes into its own. It's where you have many different types of conversion. Now you may do that with your very first upwards account, but I'm going to suggest for now, you just probably stick with the one main conversion, in which case it doesn't really matter what you do. If you're an econ business and you don't know code, then this is the point where you get somebody else involved. Because if you're an econ business, you're going to want the second option here of using different values for each conversion. What you want, if you're an econ business, is you want to pass the total sale value back into AdWords. Because if you just made a sale from a click for $500 and then the next click leads to a sale, but it's only $50, you want to know that. You want to know which clicks are leading to the $500 sales and which clicks are leading to the $50 sales and which clicks obviously are leading to no sales. So that you can then push your budget around, optimize your AdWords account, get rid of some
